refs.title: A Survey of Large Language Models
refs.why: Comprehensive survey with 4,000+ citations; covers architecture, training, and capabilities of LLMs; frequently updated to track the rapidly evolving field

refs.title: Attention Is All You Need
refs.why: The seminal transformer paper that introduced the architecture underlying all modern LLMs; 100,000+ citations, published at NeurIPS 2017

refs.title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models
refs.why: Meta's influential open-source LLM paper demonstrating that smaller models trained on more data can match larger ones; 3,800+ citations, practical reference for modern LLM design

refs.title: Neural Machine Translation of Rare Words with Subword Units
refs.why: Original BPE tokenization paper that established subword tokenization as the standard for neural language models; ~12,000 citations, ACL 2016

refs.title: Toward a Theory of Tokenization in LLMs
refs.why: First rigorous theoretical analysis of tokenization's impact on LLM behavior; provides mathematical framework for understanding tokenization effects

refs.title: Attention Is All You Need
refs.why: Introduced the self-attention mechanism that defines the context window concept in transformers; every modern LLM's context handling derives from this architecture

refs.title: LongRoPE: Extending LLM Context Window Beyond 2 Million Tokens
refs.why: First method to extend pretrained LLM context windows to 2048k tokens with minimal fine-tuning; major SOTA advancement in context length research

refs.title: A Systematic Survey of Prompt Engineering in Large Language Models
refs.why: Structured overview of prompt engineering techniques categorized by application area; covers the full taxonomy of prompting methods

refs.title: Unleashing the potential of prompt engineering in Large Language Models
refs.why: Comprehensive review of prompt engineering approaches with practical taxonomies; surveys techniques across multiple LLM families and tasks

refs.title: vllm-project/vllm
refs.why: Most widely used open-source LLM inference engine (75,000+ stars); developed at UC Berkeley Sky Computing Lab; industry standard for high-throughput serving

refs.title: ggml-org/llama.cpp
refs.why: Leading local LLM inference engine (119,000+ stars); enables LLM inference on consumer hardware via CPU/GPU quantization

refs.title: sgl-project/sglang
refs.why: High-performance serving framework from LMSYS with RadixAttention and structured generation; NeurIPS 2024 paper

refs.title: Efficient Memory Management for LLM Serving with PagedAttention
refs.why: The vLLM paper presenting PagedAttention; published at SOSP 2023 with 7,500+ citations; foundational work defining modern LLM serving architecture

refs.title: SGLang: Efficient Execution of Structured Language Model Programs
refs.why: Published at NeurIPS 2024; introduces structured LLM program execution with RadixAttention for KV cache reuse

refs.title: vLLM Documentation
refs.why: Official documentation of the most widely deployed open-source LLM serving system; covers API reference, deployment, and performance tuning

refs.title: Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models
refs.why: Foundational paper establishing power-law scaling of model performance with parameters, dataset size, and compute; essential for understanding the role of model parameters

refs.title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models
refs.why: Meta's paper documenting models from 7B to 65B parameters; concrete reference for how parameter count relates to capability, memory, and compute

refs.title: What are LLM parameters?
refs.why: IBM's official explainer distinguishing weights, biases, and hyperparameters; authoritative enterprise perspective

refs.title: Loading models — HuggingFace Transformers
refs.why: Official HuggingFace documentation on loading pretrained model checkpoints via from_pretrained(); the standard API across the open-source LLM ecosystem

refs.title: Uploading models — HuggingFace Hub
refs.why: Official documentation for uploading and sharing model checkpoints on HuggingFace Hub, the primary repository for LLM weight distribution

refs.title: Models — HuggingFace Transformers API
refs.why: API reference for PreTrainedModel base classes covering saving/loading checkpoints from local files and HuggingFace

refs.title: The Curious Case of Neural Text Degeneration
refs.why: Original Nucleus (top-p) sampling paper published at ICLR 2020; ~2,000 citations; the standard reference for truncation sampling methods in LLMs

refs.title: The Effect of Sampling Temperature on Problem Solving in LLMs
refs.why: Empirical study quantifying how temperature affects LLM reasoning performance; rigorous analysis of a widely used parameter

refs.title: Foundations of Top-k Decoding For Language Models
refs.why: First rigorous theoretical analysis of top-k sampling; establishes mathematical foundations for truncation sampling in LLMs

refs.title: CrossEntropyLoss — PyTorch 2.12 Documentation
refs.why: Official PyTorch documentation defining logits as 'predicted unnormalized scores'; the standard deep learning framework's authoritative definition

refs.title: What are logits? — TensorFlow/StackOverflow
refs.why: Canonical explanation of logits vs softmax with official TensorFlow team answer; widely referenced across the ML community
